incorrects.description: Student selected A (3 × 5 = 15), which is a true equation

steps.text: 3 times 5 is 15. Both sides match, so this equation is true. We need the false one.

incorrects.description: Student selected B (15 = 3 × 5), which is a true equation

steps.text: 15 equals 3 times 5. Both sides have the same value, so this is true. Look for the equation where the sides don't match.

incorrects.description: Student selected D (15 = 5 × 3), which is a true equation

steps.text: 5 times 3 is 15. The sides match. A false equation has sides that don't equal each other. Which one?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Equation C highlights, showing the mismatch between 3 × 5 (15) and 20.

steps.text: Let me show you. 3 times 5 is 15, not 20. The left side gives us 15, but the right side says 20. They don't match, so this equation is false. That's equation C.
